chunk_summary: Micronutrient supplementation in SAM involves timing, therapeutic food use, vitamin A indications, and delayed iron initiation to minimize infection risks during stabilization.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of therapeutic foods (F-75, F-100, RUTF) in micronutrient delivery for SAM management.
Learn the indications for vitamin A megadosing and its age-specific dosages.
Recognize the importance of delaying iron supplementation until rehabilitation readiness is achieved.
Identify protocols for folic acid, zinc, copper, and multivitamin supplementation in SAM.
key_facts: Folic acid: 5 mg on day 1, then 1 mg daily.
Zinc: 2 mg/kg/day and copper: 0.3 mg/kg/day for at least 2 weeks.
Vitamin A: High-dose based on age bands (50,000 IU <6 months; 100,000 IU 6–12 months; 200,000 IU >12 months) for deficiency or measles.
Iron: Delay until appetite returns and weight gain is established; start elemental iron at 3 mg/kg/day after 2 days of catch-up feeding.
Avoid repeat vitamin A dosing within a month unless indicated by protocol.
reference_quote: Treat vitamin A as an indication-based pathway rather than routine megadosing when therapeutic foods are used. (WHO, Pocket book of hospital care for children: severe acute malnutrition chapter)
document_key_topics: Iron delay and infection risk management
Therapeutic food use in SAM treatment
